channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: Soldiers, NDLEA arrest three suspected terrorist drug suppliers in Katsina

item.description:

item.dc:creator: ESPN Soccer
item.description: Argentina's remarkable comeback win against Egypt was all down to Lionel Messi, who is seemingly dragging his team to another final.

item.title: Kwara community celebrates return of abducted worshippers

item.title: 2027: Oyo ex-Deputy governor, Arapaja dumps Makinde, meets APC chieftains
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Reports reaching DAILY POST from the camp of a former deputy governor of Oyo State, Alhaji Taofeek Arapaja indicate that Arapaja has perfected plans to join All Progressives Congress, APC, ahead of the 2027 general elections.  DAILY POST gathered that the planned defection is coming a few hours

item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: Experts in Biochemistry and Molecular Biology have identified precision medicine, Artificial Intelligence (AI)-driven research and increased investment in science and innovation as essential to addressing Africa’s healthcare, food security and sustainable development challenges. This consensus

item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The Federal Government has announced a delay in the fourth evacuation flight scheduled to repatriate stranded Nigerians from South Africa after a technical fault was discovered on the aircraft assigned for the operation. item.title: Lagos-Abuja airfares soar above N500,000 amid surge in passenger demand
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: AIRFARES on Nigeria’s busiest domestic route, Lagos-Abuja, surged sharply on Monday and Tuesday with some airlines charging more than N500,000 for a one-way economy ticket as demand for travel to the Federal Capital Territory, Abuja outpaced available seat capacity. The post Lagos-Abuja airfares soa

item.title: Samsung will launch its new wide foldable on July 22nd
item.dc:creator: The Verge
item.description: Samsung has announced that its next Galaxy Unpacked launch event will be held on July 22nd, with the tagline: "A new shape unfolds." It's long been rumored that Samsung is about to expand its foldable phone line to a third format, with a shorter and wider version of its big book-style foldables, to

item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: The US military says it hit missile sites and command centres while Iran says it attacked US bases.

item.description: The Chief Justice of Nigeria (CJN), Justice Kudirat Kekere-Ekun, has warned newly appointed judges of the lower courts against accepting unsolicited gifts, cautioning that such actions could expose them to ethical breaches and serious consequences. item.title: World Cup 2026: Argentina players give coach Lionel Scaloni nickname
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Argentina head coach, Lionel Scaloni, has revealed that his players gave him a nickname called ‘cry baby’ because he is too emotional. Scaloni stated this to reporters after Argentina came from a 2-0 down to beat Egypt 3-2 in the World Cup Round of 16 encounter played on Tuesday. &#

item.title: 2027: Kwankwaso Counters Sheriff, Says Obi Has Northern Support

item.description: The vice-presidential candidate of the Nigeria Democratic Congress (NDC), Senator Rabiu Kwankwaso, has criticised former Borno State Governor, Senator Ali Modu Sheriff, over his comments on the electoral prospects of NDC presidential candidate, Peter Obi in Northern Nigeria. Kwankwaso, who is the vi

item.title: Sokoto: Tangaza LG Chairman Dumps APC, Resigns Position
item.dc:creator: New Telegraph
item.description: The Chairman of Tangaza Local Government Council in Sokoto State, Hon. Isa Salihu Kalenjine, has announced his resignation from the All Progressives Congress (APC) and from his position. This development was contained in a resignation letter dated May 5, 2026, and issued to newsmen on Tuesday night

item.title: FG Distributes Fertilisers, Farm Inputs To 5,600 Gombe Farmers
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: No fewer than 5,642 smallholder farmers in Gombe State have begun receiving fertilisers and other agricultural inputs under the Federal Government’s Renewed Hope Smallholder Support and Value Chain Fund (RH-SVCF). The intervention which was flagged off in Gombe on Tuesday, is part of a move to

item.title: Enzo Fernández breaks silence on Chelsea Future after Argentina's win over Egypt

Fernández, who scored the decisive stoppage-time winner to send Argentina into the quart

item.title: German Bonds Slide as Higher Oil Prices Reignite Inflation Fears
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Germany’s benchmark bond yields climbed above 3% for the first time in nearly a month as a flare-up in fighting in the Middle East lifted oil prices and inflation expectations.

item.title: Kenya lets families block relatives from gambling under proposed rules
item.dc:creator: TechCabal
item.description: The proposals, published in regulations gazetted on June 30, would also allow betting companies to suspend customers they believe are gambling beyond their financial means.

item.title: Aselsan CEO Says Global Defense Orders Are Surging
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Turkey’s $37 billion state-run defense contractor Aselsan Elektronik Sanayi ve Ticaret AS said international orders have doubled in the past year on the back of demand fueled by the conflicts in Ukraine and the Middle East. Aselsan CEO Ahmet Akyol spoke Tuesday with Bloomberg's Annmarie Hordern at t

item.title: Alphabet Powers 44% Jump in India Foreign Investment, UN Says
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Foreign direct investment in India surged in 2025, driven largely by Alphabet Inc.’s data center project, though a decline in greenfield investments signaled a weaker investment outlook, according to the United Nations’ trade agency.

item.title: India Fat-Loss Drug Sales Slow After Early Spike From Generics
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Growth in India’s obesity- and diabetes-drug market is losing steam just months after a surge triggered by the arrival of generic semaglutide products.

item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Traders are buying insurance against renewed weakness in the euro as climbing crude oil prices threaten last week’s rebound.

item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [CAF] Egypt's FIFA World Cup dream ended in heartbreaking fashion on Tuesday after the Pharaohs produced a heroic performance before eventually succumbing 3-2 to defending champions Argentina in a dramatic Round of 16 encounter in Atlanta.

item.description: A group of Ugandan farmers will file a lawsuit in the UK High Court against the East African nation’s crude pipeline, potentially delaying exports. Bloomberg's Jennifer Zabasajja reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

item.title: Broken By Design
item.dc:creator: Leadership
item.description: Every few years, the world rediscovers Africa. Sometimes it is because of a famine; at other times, it is because of a coup, a debt crisis, an epidemic, or a migration emergency. The accompanying narrative is almost always the same: Africa is broken. It is a familiar diagnosis, repeated so often tha

item.title: Access Bank, Fifth Chukker, UNICEF Renew Commitment to Expanding Educational Opportunities for Nigeria’s Most Vulnerable Children
item.dc:creator: ThisDay
item.description: The transformative power of collective action in expanding access to education took centre stage in London as Access Bank UK, Fifth Chukker, UNICEF and the Kaduna State Government reaffirmed their

item.title: NATO summit live: Trump, world leaders meet in Turkiye’s Ankara
item.dc:creator: Al Jazeera
item.description: The second and final day of the key gathering is under way in Turkiye's capital.

item.title: Ramaphosa’s visit to Ghana halted over anti-migrant tensions
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: Ghana has postponed a planned visit by South African President Cyril Ramaphosa following rising diplomatic tensions triggered by anti-migrant protests in South Africa and the repatriation of hundreds of Ghanaian nationals. The post Ramaphosa’s visit to Ghana halted over anti-migrant tensions a

item.title: Fake presidential council: Ex-DG vows to submit all documents to DSS, police
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: The former Director-General of the alleged Presidential Foreign Intervention Promotion Council (PFIPC), Adeyemi, has pledged to submit all documents in his possession to security agencies to assist ongoing investigations into the controversial agency. The post Fake presidential council: Ex-DG vows t

item.title: 2026 World Cup: Why I cried after Argentina defeated Egypt – Messi
item.dc:creator: Vanguard Sports
item.description: Argentina captain Lionel Messi has explained why he broke down in tears after his side secured a dramatic 3-2 victory over Egypt to reach the quarter-finals of the 2026 FIFA World Cup. The post 2026 World Cup: Why I cried after Argentina defeated Egypt – Messi appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.title: Why I cried after Argentina’s World Cup comeback — Messi
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: “For the record, in his first outing on the presidential ballot, Mr Obi secured approximately 2.8 million votes in the region — a remarkable achievement that cannot be dismissed lightly,” Kwankwaso said.

item.title: Xenergi launches N76 million mandatory takeover bid for Premier Paint shares
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: Xenergi Plc has launched a mandatory takeover offer to acquire up to 2 million ordinary shares of Premier Paint Plc at N38.00 per share, in a transaction valued at N76 million. The post Xenergi launches N76 million mandatory takeover bid for Premier Paint shares appeared first on Nairametrics.

item.title: Court sentences Okada rider to life imprisonment for raping nursing mother in Akwa Ibom
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Akwa Ibom State High court sitting in Uyo, on Tuesday, sentenced one Micheal Effiong Japhet, a commercial motorcycle rider to life imprisonment for raping a married woman and nursing mother in Uyo. Japhet was convicted and sentenced by Hon. Justice Archibong Archibong, after finding him guilty of ra

item.title: Africa: All of Africa Today - July 8, 2026
item.dc:creator: AllAfrica
item.description: [allAfrica]

item.title: Tambuwal’s ally joins APC after losing Sokoto ADC governorship ticket
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: Sa'idu Ubandoma, the 2023 governorship candidate of the Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) in the state, lost the ADC ticket to Manir Dan'Iya - Mr Tambuwal's former deputy. The post Tambuwal’s ally joins APC after losing Sokoto ADC governorship ticket appeared first on Premium Times Nigeria.

item.title: Starlink freezes new sign-ups in seven Kenyan counties
item.dc:creator: Techpoint Africa
item.description: On Techpoint Digest, we discuss how Starlink has frozen new sign-ups in seven Kenyan counties, how Andrea Aid wants to improve medical crowdfunding, and how South Africans claim Facebook is restricting accounts without warning.

item.title: Waterford Castle chases first success at Yarmouth
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: We have a busy day's racing on Wednesday with flat action from Chepstow and Yarmouth live on Sky Sports Racing...

item.title: President Tinubu Inaugurates Presidential Working Group On National Policing Bill
item.dc:creator: SDK Celeb
item.description: President Bola Ahmed Tinubu on Tuesday inaugurated the Presidential Working Group on the National Policing Bill to prepare the legal framework for the implementation of state police across the country.The President, represented by his Chief of Staff Femi Gbajabiamila, inaugurated the panel at the Pr
